message:
fix(fr-persist): decode upstream stream groups

Close frankenredis-qi6z.

In addition to AOF/RDB plumbing, the repo has persist_replay conformance, runtime load_aof round-trip tests, and RDB snapshot read/write coverage. Update the RDB/AOF row to reflect the implemented/tested baseline without overstating full persistence parity.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:02:59.408234137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:03:59.425531686Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T05:03:59.425332549Z","close_reason":"Reconciled persistence parity docs with replay and snapshot test coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-qet9","title":"Fix INFO keyspace to report per-database stats and real pubsub counts","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-02T19:51:28.905361065Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T19:51:34.840189727Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T19:51:34.840000108Z","close_reason":"INFO keyspace section now reports per-database key/expires counts for all 16 databases (was only reporting db0 aggregate). INFO stats pubsub_channels/pubsub_patterns/pubsub_shardchannels now use real values from Store subscriptions instead of hardcoded 0.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-qhid","title":"Implement metatables (getmetatable/setmetatable) for Lua 5.1 compatibility","description":"# Lua Metatables

## What
Metatables are a key Lua 5.1 feature. Some Redis Lua scripts use them for operator overloading or custom table behavior. Currently missing from the hand-written interpreter.

## Why
While rare in typical Redis scripts, metatables are part of the Lua 5.1 spec. Their absence could break third-party Lua libraries that depend on them.

## Implementation
1. Add a metatable field to LuaTable
2. Implement getmetatable()/setmetatable() global functions
3. Implement __index, __newindex, __tostring, __eq, __lt, __le, __add, __sub, __mul, __div, __mod, __pow, __concat, __len, __call metamethods
4. Dispatch to metamethods when operations fail on the base table

## Scope
This is significant work (~200-400 lines). Prioritize __index and __tostring as they are most commonly used.

## Files
- crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:13:08.354875762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:10:45.092683154Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:10:45.092404106Z","close_reason":"Implemented getmetatable/setmetatable with real metatable storage (Option<LuaTable> in LuaTableInner). Added __index metamethod support via get_with_index() for table subscript, field access, and method calls. Supports chained __index (table→table). Added 5 tests covering get/set, __index fallback, chain, priority, and nil removal.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankenredis-qi6z","title":"s5su part d: stream decoder extensions for LISTPACKS_2 (19) + LISTPACKS_3 (21) — consumer groups + seen/active times","description":"Extend the s5su-c decoder to parse consumer-group + consumer + PEL state from upstream type-19 and type-21 stream records. Populates the groups vec on RdbValue::Stream.

Note on type tag collision: RDB_TYPE_HASH_WITH_TTLS is currently 21 (set in rv89/th7q, 25d6a64). This conflicts with upstream's RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 which is also 21. Options:
  * Option A: Shift our internal HashWithTtls to a reserved range (e.g., 100+) so upstream 21 can be our decoder-only stream path.
  * Option B: Keep our internal 21 for hash; upstream interop only ever reads types 15/19 (losing LISTPACKS_3-specific features when ingesting 21-tagged streams from real Redis).

Plan: adopt Option A. Revise th7q's type byte to 100, document the change, re-run th7q tests. Then use 21 exclusively for the stream decoder in upstream-produced files.

Blocks s5su-e.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:17:24.348682901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:17:24.348682901Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-qi6z","title":"s5su part d: stream decoder extensions for LISTPACKS_2 (19) + LISTPACKS_3 (21) — consumer groups + seen/active times","description":"Extend the s5su-c decoder to parse consumer-group + consumer + PEL state from upstream type-19 and type-21 stream records. Populates the groups vec on RdbValue::Stream.

Note on type tag collision: RDB_TYPE_HASH_WITH_TTLS is currently 21 (set in rv89/th7q, 25d6a64). This conflicts with upstream's RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 which is also 21. Options:
  * Option A: Shift our internal HashWithTtls to a reserved range (e.g., 100+) so upstream 21 can be our decoder-only stream path.
  * Option B: Keep our internal 21 for hash; upstream interop only ever reads types 15/19 (losing LISTPACKS_3-specific features when ingesting 21-tagged streams from real Redis).

Plan: adopt Option A. Revise th7q's type byte to 100, document the change, re-run th7q tests. Then use 21 exclusively for the stream decoder in upstream-produced files.

Blocks s5su-e.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:17:24.348682901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T02:21:25.307484050Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T02:21:25.305627195Z","close_reason":"Decoded upstream stream type 21 groups and PEL ownership; moved hash-with-TTL tag to private 100","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-qk7b","title":"Classify bounded AOF final-tail replay repair","description":"FR-P2C-005 Stage D5 building block: add fr-persist replay-tail repair classification for C06/C07/C14. The helper should keep non-final corrupt/truncated AOF segments fail-closed, allow only bounded final-segment truncation to the last valid command offset, and return structured policy/evidence data that later runtime wiring can log with persist.replay.tail_truncate_recover / persist.replay.repair_policy_applied / persist.replay.nonfinal_truncation_fatal.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T06:05:29.592014168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T06:27:42.097116159Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T06:27:42.095292220Z","close_reason":"Completed bounded final-tail AOF replay repair classifier; validated via rch fmt/check/clippy/fr-persist tests/conformance","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["persistence","phase2c","upstream-parity"]}
